exercises.title: Build BV Oracle
exercises.description: Create an oracle for the hidden string s = '1011'.

    return oracle

exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def inner_product_mod2(s, x):
    result = 0
    for i in range(len(s)):
        result ^= s[i] * ((x >> i) & 1)
    return result

def create_oracle(s):
    n = len(s)
    N = 2**n
    oracle = np.zeros((N, N))
    
    for x in range(N):
        fx = inner_product_mod2(s, x)
        oracle[x, x] = (-1)**fx
    
    return oracle

s = [1, 0, 1, 1]
oracle = create_oracle(s)
print("Oracle diagonal:", np.diag(oracle))

exercises.title: Run Complete BV Algorithm

exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

H = np.array([[1, 1], [1, -1]]) / np.sqrt(2)

def tensor_H(n):
    result = np.array([[1]])
    for _ in range(n):
        result = np.kron(result, H)
    return result

def bv_algorithm(oracle, n):
    N = 2**n
    Hn = tensor_H(n)
    
    state = np.ones(N) / np.sqrt(N)
    state = oracle @ state
    state = Hn @ state
    
    idx = np.argmax(np.abs(state)**2)
    return format(idx, f'0{n}b')[::-1]  # Reverse for bit ordering

print("Hidden string found!")
hints: Apply H^n before and after oracle
